markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close ED-WB-011 (DEC-007 reverses descope) HONESTLY: a Debug Adapter Protocol (DAP) client core in Handshake (sessions, breakpoints, stack/scopes/variables, step/continue/pause, debug console) + FIRST concrete adapter: Node.js via its built-in inspector protocol (no external adapter binary). Adapter registry is extensible (future: Python debugpy, lldb) - registry entries beyond Node are configuration, NOT stub UI; ship ONLY what runs. UI: breakpoint gutter in Monaco blocks + debug side panel + console.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
NO mock debug sessions: every shipped adapter must drive a real process.
Breakpoint hit, variable inspection, stepping must be real-runtime proven on a real Node script.
Adapters not yet implemented must be absent from the UI (no dead dropdown entries).
scope.expected_behavior: Operator sets a breakpoint in a Node script, launches, hits it, inspects variables, steps, continues - all inside Handshake.
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end Node debug session runtime-proven (launch -> breakpoint hit -> variables -> step -> continue -> exit).
Debug console eval in paused frame proven.
No non-functional adapter entries visible (negative check).
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils (scoped) green with real-PG tests for backend state.
pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence: commit 03f76dcf on feat/WP-KERNEL-009 (live Node DAP debug session + GUI), zero .GOV

runtime proof: real node child under --inspect-brk breakpoint test PASS (15.12s, breakpoint verified by CDP, real Stopped/variables/step); durable per-doc breakpoints round-trip on real PG with KE- EventLedger receipts PASS (23.74s, migration 0331); live-session HTTP surface driven via curl (GET /debug/adapters -> node only [honesty gate], POST /debug/sessions -> paused, set breakpoint); offline Playwright PASS (1.2m) with REAL browser + REAL Monaco breakpoint gutter + REAL node debuggee + REAL PG; vitest 8 PASS; tsc + eslint clean

adversarial review caught a FICTIONAL claim (implement asserted 'driven through IPC kernel_dap_*' but those commands did not exist); harden then built the real IPC layer + DebugSidePanel + live session and proved it end-to-end

VALIDATION CAVEAT: breakpoint gutter ships in DebugSidePanel's own Monaco editor (operator-usable, runtime-proven); NOT yet wired into in-document MonacoCodeBlockView code blocks inside RichTextEditor (follow-up: thread breakpoint/session state through the Tiptap NodeView)
registry honesty gate proven: only Node adapter listable; no disabled/python/lldb stub entries

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-254 requires a real Node.js Debug Adapter (DAP) core inside Handshake — set breakpoint, launch a real node child, hit it, inspect variables, evaluate, step, continue, plus durable per-document breakpoint persistence and a no-dead-adapter honesty gate. Commit 03f76dcf delivers all of this with real CDP/V8-inspector process control, real PostgreSQL+EventLedger breakpoint persistence, and offline Playwright + real-PG runtime proof. The code is genuine, not scaffolding.
findings: node_inspector.rs:161-428 launch_node_session spawns a REAL `node --inspect-brk=127.0.0.1:0` child, discovers the inspector ws url from stderr, holds a persistent websocket, and demultiplexes CDP responses/events — a genuine V8 Inspector client, no mock, no external adapter binary.

node_inspector.rs:534-823 implements the full DebugAdapter trait against real CDP: setBreakpointByUrl with verified=locations-nonempty (line 567), real Debugger.paused stack/scopes, Runtime.getProperties for real variable values, evaluateOnCallFrame, stepOver/Into/Out, resume, pause, terminate with real exit-code reaping.

tests/debug_adapter_node_session.rs:40-135 is a real end-to-end runtime proof: launches real node, asserts breakpoint binds (verified==true via CDP), stops at line 2, reads REAL a=2/b=40, evaluates a+b==42, steps to line 3, continues to real exit 0; panics ENVIRONMENT_BLOCKED if node absent (no false green).

tests/debug_adapter_breakpoint_persistence.rs:32-157 proves durable per-doc breakpoint round-trip on REAL Handshake-managed PostgreSQL; line 37-40 the test fails loudly (no silent skip) when PG is absent, satisfying the DEC-007 real-PG mandate.

postgres.rs:9199-9286 set_debug_breakpoints runs a real transaction: appends a real kernel event via append_kernel_event_with_executor and persists breakpoints with FK event_ledger_event_id; the KE- receipt id is produced by the ledger, not fabricated — migration 0331 (line 29) enforces the FK so a fake receipt would fail insert.

migrations/0331_debug_breakpoints.sql creates knowledge_debug_breakpoints with real FKs to knowledge_rich_documents, workspaces, and kernel_event_ledger plus a unique (doc,source_url,line) constraint — durable PostgreSQL authority, no SQLite.

registry.rs:27-34 listable_adapters() returns ONLY Node (runnable), and api/debug_adapter.rs:151-157 GET /debug/adapters returns exactly that list — the negative honesty-gate check; no python/lldb/disabled stub entries. Playwright spec lines 193-195 assert exactly one adapter option.

tests/visual/mt254-node-debug-session-real-backend.spec.ts is a genuine offline E2E: starts the real cargo mt254_debug_session_fixture backend, blocks all external network (line 163-178, asserts externalRequests==[] at 282), drives a REAL Monaco breakpoint gutter, hits a REAL node breakpoint, reads real vars, evals 42, steps, continues to exit 0, and PUTs breakpoints proven back from real PG with a KE- receipt (lines 271-279).

The prior FAIL (adversarial review) was a FICTIONAL 'driven through IPC kernel_dap_*' claim; grep confirms kernel_dap_ no longer exists and the comments were corrected to the real HTTP surface — the prior failure is genuinely fixed.

Honestly-documented caveat (MT evidence line): the breakpoint gutter ships in DebugSidePanel's own Monaco editor, not yet wired into in-document MonacoCodeBlockView code blocks. This does not contradict the MT acceptance criteria (operator-usable gutter + panel + console + E2E Node debug are all proven); it is a disclosed follow-on, not a deferred-live escape on the required path.
Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): MT cites spec v02.193/v02.192 anchors while current is v02.194; section numbers resolve identically. Not a failing reason per review ground rules.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
